hypothesis: Contrastive multimodal pretraining between SMILES and electron density grids will induce emergent enantiomer discrimination—distinguishing R from S stereoisomers that share identical SMILES strings but differ in 3D spatial arrangement—if the symbolic anchor is replaced with stereochemistry-aware InChI descriptors that encode chirality, such that the contrastive loss forces the 3D encoder to embed mirror-image molecules in separable latent regions without any explicit chirality-aware architectural priors.
